description_long: This object is a vintage-style computer monitor with a thick, rectangular frame and a prominent, deep-set screen. The bezel is wide and angular, forming a bold border around the display area, and the overall body is constructed from a light-colored, matte material that appears to be plastic or composite. The sides and rear of the monitor are solid and boxy, with subtle texturing visible on the side panels, possibly for ventilation. The monitor sits atop a sturdy, rectangular pedestal base that provides stability but does not appear to offer any adjustability. No buttons, ports, or branding are visible on any surface, giving the monitor a minimalist and utilitarian appearance. The design is reminiscent of older CRT monitors commonly found in public or industrial urban environments.
description: A vintage, boxy computer monitor with a thick, light-colored bezel and a deep-set screen, mounted on a solid rectangular base. The exterior is matte and unadorned, with no visible controls or branding.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, angular bezel framing the dark screen and the rectangular base beneath.
description_view_1: Left side view revealing the monitor's deep, boxy profile and subtle side panel texturing.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, unbroken back panel and the full width of the base.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, highlighting the monitor's depth and the plain, textured side surface.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, angular, and made of matte light-colored plastic, with sharp corners and no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: Rectangular pedestal base, solid and flat, constructed from the same material as the monitor body, with no visible adjustability features.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on any side of the monitor.
